title: glyceria procumbens
intro: Glyceria procumbens, also known as creek sedge or small-fruited mannagrass, is a perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to North America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and other infections. The health benefits of Glyceria procumbens include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and blood pressure. Glyceria procumbens is typically prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and can be used internally or applied topically to various health issues.
